Cell.Data: The DEV TEAM / MISC NOTES column contains info on what text between { } signs means (if applicable). It usually stands for either a numeric value, or a numeric value with an icon. Text between [ ] signs stands for a certain type of icon.
Planet name; Imperial as in belonging to the Star Union. Default as in standard world to play in
Świat imperialny nadający się do ponownej kolonizacji.
Planet name; Frontier as in at the edge, uncolonized
Feral, barely colonized world at the edge of the Star Union.
Dziki, słabo skolonizowany świat na skraju terytorium Związku Gwiezdnego.
Planet name; Capital as in main hub/planet
Former population centers with bountiful Imperial remains.
Dawne skupiska ludności z licznymi pozostałościami po imperium.
Planet name; as in heavily focused on factories and production
Planeta obfitująca w surowce, z pozostałościami przemysłu.
Refers to a male emperor of the Star Union
Translate, forum as in democratic gathering
Translate, Pia is a male name
Translate, Dio is a male name
Refers to a male emperor of the Star Union
Translate, Komen is a female name
Translate, can split "Spirit" and "Rise" if required
Translate, Jorat is a male name
Translate, Idan is a male name
Translate, Ghett is a male name
Translate, can split "Born" and "Stone" if required
Translate, Balmut is a male name
Translate, can split "Melt" and "Core" if required
Devastated by the cataclysm or ancient wars.
Planeta spustoszona przez kataklizm lub pradawne wojny.
Name of a world / planet template that can be selected when the player is customizing the type of planet they want to play on.
A virtually constructed world for fledgling Commanders to practice.
Wirtualny świat zbudowany w celu szkolenia świeżo upieczonych dowódców.
Description of the world/ planet template. Used as flavor and to give the player an idea of what the gameplay on such a planet is like. EDIT: added full stop, no reloca required
